2026-06-17-v1.49-narrative-indexes.title: Intermittent flaky test `fallback_warn_includes_chapter_field` (review_report) fails under full parallel `cargo test -p nexus-orchestration`; passes in isolation. Pre-existing on iteration/v1.49 base.
2026-06-17-v1.49-narrative-indexes.source: P1 implementer (pre-existing flake, verified against base per .mstar/AGENTS.md protocol); QC3 also verified on origin/main @ be27111b
2026-06-17-v1.49-narrative-indexes.note: Verified pre-existing per the repo's 'Pre-existing claim verification protocol': reproduces on iteration/v1.49 @ 3630a4e5 (base) with `cargo test -p nexus-orchestration` (full parallel suite — 9 passed, 1 failed in review_report); passes with `cargo test -p nexus-orchestration --test review_report` (in isolation, parallel and serial). QC3 also verified on origin/main @ be27111b with 2/10 failure rate. Cause: global tracing registry contention when many test binaries install/inspect the subscriber concurrently. Not caused by V1.49 P1 — review_report.rs and its capture layer are untouched. Suggested fix: scoped subscriber guard or serializing that single test.
2026-06-17-v1.49-narrative-indexes.closure_note: V1.50 P-last T1: marked every test in tests/review_report.rs with #[serial_test::serial] so the whole binary runs mutually exclusively (equivalent to --test-threads=1 for this binary; other binaries still parallelise). Diagnosis via eprintln probes confirmed the function fired its warn!/info! events on the calling thread with persisted_count=1 in both passing and failing runs — the capture layer was intermittently dropping the first event after install when sibling tests competed for the tokio blocking-thread pool that sqlx uses for SQLite I/O. Added serial_test = "3" to nexus-orchestration [dev-dependencies]. Verified 30/30 clean at --test-threads=8 (was ~3/30 failing), 5/5 at --test-threads=16, 5/5 cross-binary full suite. Also pinned the flaky test to #[tokio::test(flavor = "current_thread")] as defence-in-depth for the set_default guard's lifetime across .await.

2026-06-18-v1.50-cron-foundation.title: TZ silently clobbered on role-only patch operations (data consistency)
2026-06-18-v1.50-cron-foundation.source: qc1 (architecture/maintainability) W1; corroborated by qc2/qc3 (no disagreement on the data-loss path)
2026-06-18-v1.50-cron-foundation.note: Trigger: `set my-work --no-review` (or any patch omitting --tz) on a Work with custom TZ stored. handle_set folds env/default TZ into effective_args.tz unconditionally, then apply_set_args patches schedule.tz = resolved_tz, clobbering the previously-configured author TZ. Suggested fix: only fold env/default TZ when user explicitly resets (no-flags → defaults branch) or passes --tz; for patch operations without --tz leave base.tz unchanged. Recommend extracting TZ resolution into a pure helper (resolve_tz) for testability. Add regression test set_no_review_preserves_custom_tz.
2026-06-18-v1.50-cron-foundation.closure_note: V1.50 T-A P0 fix-wave: `resolve_tz(args_tz, env) -> Option<String>` pure helper extracted; `handle_set` only folds env/default TZ on explicit reset or --tz; for role-only patch operations, base.tz is preserved. Regression test `set_no_review_preserves_custom_tz` passes; 3 unit tests cover the pure helper.

2026-06-18-v1.50-cron-foundation.title: Spec §3.1 'all-off' rule not enforced (spec compliance)
2026-06-18-v1.50-cron-foundation.scope: crates/nexus42/src/commands/creator/works/cron.rs :: CronCommand::Set (no --all-off flag) and apply_set_args (no all-disabled check)
2026-06-18-v1.50-cron-foundation.note: Spec §3.1 verbatim: 'At least one role must remain enabled: true unless --all-off is passed (CLI rejects empty schedules).' Both --all-off flag and post-mutation check missing. `set my-work --no-brainstorm --no-write --no-review` succeeds silently. T-A P1 cron evaluator will then skip all fires indefinitely. Fix: add --all-off flag in CronCommand::Set clap; post-mutation count of enabled roles; if 0 and !all_off return CliError::Config with stable code (e.g. E_CRON_ALL_ROLES_DISABLED). Hermetic test apply_set_args_all_off_without_flag_rejects.
